title: Assessing Bit Digital (BTBT) Valuation As Ethereum And AI Expansion Meets Q1 2026 Earnings Volatility
text: Bit Digital reported Q1 2026 results showing US$27.92 million in quarterly revenue alongside a US$146.67 million net loss. The company's stock price has exhibited significant volatility, with a 15.26% one day share price decline contrasting against 17.97% 30 day share price gain, though the 1-year return stands at negative 24.16%. The most followed narrative from the investment community values BTBT at $5.13 per share, suggesting considerable upside from the recent $1.81 price point. Analyst consensus targets $5.70, with the most bullish projections reaching $7.00 and bearish estimates as low as $5.00. This valuation framework relies on three critical assumptions: aggressive revenue expansion, margin improvement, and application of an 8.69% discount rate. The analysis uses a lower future P/E than earlier versions of the model to justify the gap between current and fair value assessments. The investment thesis centers on Ethereum infrastructure and AI expansion initiatives. However, the analysis notes that this opportunity hinges on Ethereum concentration and reliance on equity funding, where weaker ETH pricing or heavier dilution could quickly challenge those optimistic margin and valuation assumptions.
